name: pending-stub
description: KIND-001b installs the platform foundation layer into the kind cluster, unblocking the entire PLATFORM delivery chain. HA-002/003/004 advance resilience validation in the kind cluster. DEVEX-012 enforces autarky compliance on developer portal charts.
sprintGoal: KIND-001b passes so the PLATFORM chain is unblocked; HA-002/003/004 advance kind-cluster resilience validation; DEVEX-012 clears the autarky gate for developer portal charts.

stories.title: kind bootstrap: install Cilium, cert-manager, sealed-secrets, MinIO into kind cluster
stories.description: Split of KIND-001 (achievable=3). Platform component install half: after KIND-001a produces a running 3-node kind cluster, install Cilium CNI (kindnet disabled), cert-manager, sealed-secrets, local-path-provisioner (default StorageClass), and MinIO into that cluster. Depends on KIND-001a.
acceptanceCriteria: kubectl get pods -n kube-system shows cilium pods Running (kindnet pods absent)
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager shows all Running
kubectl get pods -n sealed-secrets shows all Running
kubectl get pods -n minio shows all Running
kubectl get storageclass shows local-path as default (annotated storageclass.kubernetes.io/is-default-class: 'true')
stories.returnedReason: Split from KIND-001 (achievable=3 — too many integration points for one story)
stories.testPlan: kubectl get pods -n kube-system | grep cilium; kubectl get pods -n cert-manager; kubectl get pods -n sealed-secrets; kubectl get pods -n minio; kubectl get storageclass — show all output verbatim. All pods must show Running/Completed. local-path must be default.

smart.notes: Achievable score 4: installs 5 components (Cilium, cert-manager, sealed-secrets, local-path-provisioner, MinIO) — near the top of the 2-point budget but a focused split with clear scope.

stories.readinessNote: READY: dependency KIND-001a completed in sprint 31 (passes:true, reviewed:true in prd/increment-31-pending-stub.json).

stories.title: PDB drain validation: kubectl drain blocks eviction of last pod when minAvailable=1
stories.description: Create kind/fixtures/pdb-test.yaml defining: a Namespace (pdb-test), a Deployment with replicaCount=2, podAntiAffinity (requiredDuringScheduling across nodes), and a PodDisruptionBudget with minAvailable=1. In kind-sovereign-test, apply the fixture, drain a worker node, and verify: (a) kubectl drain exits non-zero or produces 'Cannot evict pod as it would violate the pod disruption budget' output when trying to evict the last pod, and (b) at least 1 pdb-test pod remains Running during the drain. After uncordon, verify both pods return to Running.
acceptanceCriteria: kind/fixtures/pdb-test.yaml exists and contains Namespace, Deployment (replicaCount=2), and PodDisruptionBudget (minAvailable=1) resources: python3 -c "import yaml; docs=list(yaml.safe_load_all(open('kind/fixtures/pdb-test.yaml'))); kinds={d['kind'] for d in docs if d}; assert 'Deployment' in kinds and 'PodDisruptionBudget' in kinds and 'Namespace' in kinds; print('OK')" exits 0
kubectl apply -f kind/fixtures/pdb-test.yaml --context kind-sovereign-test exits 0 and kubectl get pods -l app=pdb-test -n pdb-test --context kind-sovereign-test shows 2/2 Running within 60 seconds

kubectl drain <worker-node> --delete-emptydir-data --ignore-daemonsets --context kind-sovereign-test produces output containing 'Cannot evict pod as it would violate the pod disruption budget' OR exits non-zero
kubectl get pods -l app=pdb-test -n pdb-test --context kind-sovereign-test immediately after drain attempt shows at least 1 pod Running
kubectl uncordon <worker-node> --context kind-sovereign-test followed by kubectl wait --for=condition=Ready pod -l app=pdb-test -n pdb-test --timeout=60s --context kind-sovereign-test exits 0
stories.testPlan: kind get clusters (verify kind-sovereign-test) && kubectl get nodes --context kind-sovereign-test && kubectl apply -f kind/fixtures/pdb-test.yaml --context kind-sovereign-test && kubectl get pods -l app=pdb-test -n pdb-test --context kind-sovereign-test && WORKER=$(kubectl get nodes --context kind-sovereign-test --no-headers | grep -v control-plane | head -1 | awk '{print $1}') && kubectl drain $WORKER --delete-emptydir-data --ignore-daemonsets --context kind-sovereign-test 2>&1 | tee /tmp/drain.out && kubectl get pods -l app=pdb-test -n pdb-test --context kind-sovereign-test && kubectl uncordon $WORKER --context kind-sovereign-test; show all output verbatim

smart.notes: Achievable score 4: drain/uncordon flow requires a live kind cluster and involves multi-step orchestration (apply fixture, drain, verify, uncordon) — tight but within a 3-point budget.

stories.readinessNote: READY: dependency KIND-001a completed in sprint 31 (passes:true, reviewed:true in prd/increment-31-pending-stub.json).

stories.title: Rolling update smoke test verifies zero pod unavailability during Helm chart upgrade in kind
stories.description: Create kind/smoke-test/rolling-update.sh that: (1) installs a test Helm chart into kind-sovereign-test with replicaCount=2 and maxUnavailable=0 maxSurge=1, (2) upgrades the chart (e.g. bumps an annotation) triggering a rolling update, (3) polls kubectl get pods every 2 seconds during the rollout, (4) verifies at no point were fewer than 1 pod Running, (5) exits 0 with 'PASS: zero unavailability during rollout' if the invariant held throughout. Script must pass shellcheck -S error and support --dry-run.
acceptanceCriteria: kind/smoke-test/rolling-update.sh exists and passes shellcheck -S error: shellcheck -S error kind/smoke-test/rolling-update.sh exits 0
bash -n kind/smoke-test/rolling-update.sh exits 0
kind/smoke-test/rolling-update.sh --dry-run prints the test steps and the chart it will use, then exits 0 without executing helm install
kind/smoke-test/rolling-update.sh runs against kind-sovereign-test and exits 0 with output containing 'PASS: zero unavailability during rollout'
helm template of the test chart used confirms strategy.rollingUpdate.maxUnavailable is 0: helm template <test-chart> | grep -A5 'rollingUpdate' shows 'maxUnavailable: 0'
stories.testPlan: shellcheck -S error kind/smoke-test/rolling-update.sh && bash -n kind/smoke-test/rolling-update.sh && kind/smoke-test/rolling-update.sh --dry-run && kind/smoke-test/rolling-update.sh; show all output verbatim including the per-poll pod counts

smart.notes: Specific score 4: 'a test Helm chart' is not named or pathed — implementer must decide what chart to create. Measurable score 4: AC5 references '<test-chart>' placeholder with no concrete name, making the helm template grep command underspecified. TimeBound score 4: unspecified test chart is a scope-expansion risk — implementer could gold-plate a custom chart instead of a minimal one.

stories.readinessNote: READY: dependency KIND-001a completed in sprint 31 (passes:true, reviewed:true in prd/increment-31-pending-stub.json).

stories.title: kind 3-node cluster bootstrap script provisions control-plane HA and passes contract validate
stories.description: Create cluster/kind/ha-bootstrap.sh that provisions a kind cluster with 3 control-plane nodes and 2 worker nodes using a kind config file. After provisioning, the script must run python3 contract/validate.py against a generated cluster-values.yaml to confirm autarky invariants pass. The script must be idempotent (safe to re-run if cluster exists) and print PASS/FAIL at the end.
acceptanceCriteria: cluster/kind/ha-bootstrap.sh exists and passes shellcheck: shellcheck -S error cluster/kind/ha-bootstrap.sh exits 0

cluster/kind/kind-ha-config.yaml exists with 3 control-plane nodes: python3 -c "import yaml; c=yaml.safe_load(open('cluster/kind/kind-ha-config.yaml')); cps=[n for n in c['nodes'] if n.get('role')=='control-plane']; assert len(cps)==3, f'expected 3, got {len(cps)}'; print('OK')" exits 0 and prints OK
python3 contract/validate.py contract/v1/tests/valid.yaml exits 0 with output containing CONTRACT VALID (G7 gate passes before cluster changes)
stories.testPlan: shellcheck -S error cluster/kind/ha-bootstrap.sh; python3 -c "import yaml; c=yaml.safe_load(open('cluster/kind/kind-ha-config.yaml')); print('control-plane nodes:', len([n for n in c['nodes'] if n.get('role')=='control-plane']))"; python3 contract/validate.py contract/v1/tests/valid.yaml; show all output verbatim.

smart.notes: Specific score 4: description says '3 control-plane + 2 worker nodes' but no AC verifies the 2 worker nodes exist. Measurable score 3: description says 'run contract/validate.py against a generated cluster-values.yaml' but AC3 only validates the pre-existing static fixture (contract/v1/tests/valid.yaml) — the generated cluster-values.yaml is neither created nor validated by any AC, creating a disconnect between stated intent and verifiable criteria.

stories.readinessNote: READY: dependency KIND-001a completed in sprint 31 (passes:true, reviewed:true in prd/increment-31-pending-stub.json).

stories.title: Backstage and code-server Helm charts comply with autarky gate (G6)
stories.description: Audit all templates under platform/charts/backstage/ and platform/charts/code-server/ and replace any hard-coded external registry references (docker.io, ghcr.io, etc.) with {{ .Values.global.imageRegistry }}. Add autarky check to each chart's CI lint step. Both charts must pass the G6 grep check clean.
acceptanceCriteria: grep -rn 'docker\.io\|quay\.io\|ghcr\.io\|gcr\.io\|registry\.k8s\.io' platform/charts/backstage/templates/ returns no matches
grep -rn 'docker\.io\|quay\.io\|ghcr\.io\|gcr\.io\|registry\.k8s\.io' platform/charts/code-server/templates/ returns no matches
helm lint platform/charts/backstage/ exits 0
helm lint platform/charts/code-server/ exits 0
helm template platform/charts/backstage/ --set global.imageRegistry=harbor.example.com renders all image refs starting with harbor.example.com
stories.testPlan: bash -c 'result=$(grep -rn "docker\.io\|quay\.io\|ghcr\.io\|gcr\.io\|registry\.k8s\.io" platform/charts/backstage/templates/ platform/charts/code-server/templates/ 2>/dev/null); [ -z "$result" ] && echo PASS || (echo FAIL && echo "$result" && exit 1)'

smart.notes: Measurable score 4: AC5 ('renders all image refs starting with harbor.example.com') lacks an explicit grep command — 'helm template ... | grep -E ...' is not provided, so the check is descriptive rather than binary. Achievable score 4: auditing and patching templates in two charts may touch many files depending on how many image references exist.

stories.title: code-server chart: PersistentVolumeClaim for /home/coder so workspace survives pod restarts
stories.description: Add platform/charts/code-server/templates/pvc.yaml: a PersistentVolumeClaim for the code-server workspace. The storageClass must reference {{ .Values.global.storageClass }} (never hardcoded). Size must be configurable via values.yaml (default 5Gi). Update the Deployment template to add a volumeMount at /home/coder and a corresponding volume referencing the PVC. This ensures that agent workspaces, cloned git repos, and shell history survive pod restarts.
acceptanceCriteria: platform/charts/code-server/templates/pvc.yaml exists — ls platform/charts/code-server/templates/pvc.yaml exits 0
helm template platform/charts/code-server/ shows a PersistentVolumeClaim with storageClassName: {{ .Values.global.storageClass }}
helm template platform/charts/code-server/ shows a VolumeMount with mountPath: /home/coder
platform/charts/code-server/values.yaml has a persistence.size field — grep 'persistence:' platform/charts/code-server/values.yaml exits 0
helm lint platform/charts/code-server/ exits 0
stories.testPlan: helm lint platform/charts/code-server/ && helm template platform/charts/code-server/ | grep -A3 'PersistentVolumeClaim' && helm template platform/charts/code-server/ | grep '/home/coder' && grep 'persistence:' platform/charts/code-server/values.yaml; show all output verbatim

smart.notes: Measurable score 4: AC2 asserts 'storageClassName: {{ .Values.global.storageClass }}' but helm template renders the value, not the template syntax — the AC should specify --set global.storageClass=test-class and grep for 'storageClassName: test-class' to be binary-verifiable.
